  5. The deadlift

The deadlift

The deadlift is the last lift. You lift the bar from the floor until you are standing up straight, then wait for the signal to put it down.

This is a plain-English guide. The official IPF Technical Rules are the final word.

How to do it

  1. Face the front. The bar sits on the floor in front of your feet.
  2. Grip the bar with both hands. You can grip it any way you like.
  3. Lift the bar in one movement until you are standing up straight. There is no signal to start, you begin when you are ready.
  4. Lock your knees and pull your shoulders back. Hold the bar still.
  5. When the referee says "Down", lower the bar to the floor. Keep hold of it, do not drop it.

Once the bar leaves the floor it must not move downward until you are standing straight. If the bar settles a little as your shoulders come back, that is fine.

Why a deadlift fails

Your deadlift will not count if you:

  • let the bar move downward before you reach the top
  • do not stand up straight with your shoulders back
  • do not lock your knees at the top
  • rest or support the bar on your thighs as you lift
  • step forward, backward or sideways (rocking between heel and toe is fine)
  • lower the bar before the referee says "Down"
  • let go of the bar and drop it on the way down
